summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a stellar acoustic set highlighted by China Doll, Ripple and On the Road Again.
review: The acoustic Warfield shows from September 1980 are consistently praised for their crystal‑clear soundboard source and superb mix. Reviewers single out the expansive “China Doll”, the sparkling “On The Road Again” and the gorgeous “Ripple”, noting Jerry’s heartfelt vocals and tasteful guitar work, while Brent’s piano adds a fresh sparkle to the acoustic arrangements.

ai_rating.rationale: All eight reviewers rate the set 4–5 stars, emphasizing both high‑quality performance and excellent recording; the weighted average yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers describe the sound as good to great, crisp, phenomenal and flawless; no issues with muddiness or drop‑outs were reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The acoustic set is viewed as a cohesive, gold‑standard performance that flows beautifully; reviewers note the electric portions are weaker but the focus here is on the consistently strong acoustic material.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar riffs (especially on China Doll) and vocal delivery are highlighted as heartfelt, tasteful and full of wistful fire.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s piano work is praised for replacing the electric keyboard sound with a real piano that sounds fabulous and harmonizes nicely.
